figures.caption: Figure 6: Qualitative evidence supporting the use of Δ𝑆𝑖𝑡 as a proxy for timestep contribution. The figure shows a clear correlation between Δ𝑆𝑖𝑡 and Δ𝑅𝑖𝑡 .
Figure 9: More qualitative comparison of video generation. For each text prompt, the generated videos are shown from top to bottom using Wan2.2, DanceGRPO, and our proposed method, respectively.

Figure 3: Qualitative comparison on image generation. Our method produces images with higher fidelity and aesthetic quality compared to existing GRPO-based approaches, demonstrating clearer structures and more consistent temporal alignment.
Figure 8: More qualitative comparison of video generation. For each text prompt, the generated videos are shown from top to bottom using Wan2.2, DanceGRPO, and our proposed method, respectively.

Figure 7: More qualitative comparison of image generation. For each text prompt, the generated videos are shown from top to bottom using Flux1.0-dev, VIPO, DanceGRPO, and our proposed method, respectively.

Figure 4: Qualitative comparison on video generation. From top to bottom: results generated by Wan2.2 (baseline), DanceGRPO, and our proposed OTCA. Our method produces more detailed and perceptually pleasing results, with richer textures, more accurate colors, and better semantic alignment to the prompt.

Figure 2: Overview of our method. Different from existing GRPO-based approaches, our pipeline explicitly injects multiple objective optimization signals into each temporal timestep dimension, enabling balanced optimization at the timestep level.
